prospects.name: Gaurav Sharma
prospects.notes: Initial contact via LinkedIn. Technically literate. PCI-DSS focus.

tasks.title: Production Dockerfile full build
tasks.description: Bake qwen3:4b + qwen2.5-coder:1.5b into image. Test --network none end-to-end.

tasks.title: Identify 2-3 fresh VP InfoSec targets
tasks.description: Private banks, NBFCs, payment aggregators. LinkedIn outreach.

tasks.title: Run Juice Shop Tier 3 full scan
tasks.description: Non-sandboxed. Full Agent 3/4 LLM deep analysis pass.

notes.title: Venture Dock Meeting — Filed
notes.content:
Met with CEO. Impressed by architecture, no technical objections.

Verdict: too early, cybersec program offline, wrong geography.

Standing invitation: contact when raising a funding round or going global.

Action: Do not follow up until first paying client is closed.

notes.title: BFSI Sales Call Framework
notes.content:
Opening: Air-gap story — 90 seconds, then demo.

"Every time a bank runs a source code audit, the code goes somewhere. ThreatScope inverts this."

Demo: Juice Shop Tier 1/2 — 251 findings, 0 vendor FP.

Framing: Use compliance language. Not CVE numbers.

One ask: CISO introduction only. Nothing else.

templates.name: Cold LinkedIn DM — VP InfoSec
templates.content:
Hi [Name],

You're dealing with a problem most banks don't talk about publicly: every time a source code audit happens, the code leaves the network.

ThreatScope inverts that. Local LLM inference, bundled in Docker, zero egress. Air-gapped from day one.

We ran it on a 605-file Node.js app — 251 findings, 0 false positives on vendor code. Each finding maps to your RBI Master Direction obligations.

Worth 20 minutes to see what it found?

— Adithya

templates.name: Cold Email — BFSI
templates.content:
Subject: Source code security that stays on your servers — 251 findings, air-gapped

Hi [Name],

[Company]'s source code is your most sensitive asset. Every cloud-based scanner is a data sovereignty risk under RBI Master Direction 4.4.

ThreatScope is the only AI security scanner that never leaves your network.

Would you be comfortable with a 48-hour Risk Assessment on one of your repos?

— Adithya Poojary
ThreatScope

templates.name: 48-Hour PoV Proposal
templates.content:
48-Hour Risk Assessment — ThreatScope

Prospect: [Company Name]
Contact: [Name, Role]

Scope: [Payment Gateway / Auth Service / Mobile Banking API]
Duration: 48 hours from access grant to report delivery

Deliverables:
- Risk Snapshot: 3-5 critical business logic findings
- Compliance mapping: RBI Master Direction 4.x + PCI-DSS 6.5.x
- Zero-egress verification

Pricing: ₹3-5L, credited toward first annual contract.

ThreatScope — the only AI security scanner that never leaves your network.

templates.name: CISO Objection Responses
templates.content:
Q: What happens to our source code?
A: Nothing leaves your network. Ever.

Q: How does it compare to SonarQube?
A: SonarQube catches patterns it was programmed to know. ThreatScope catches business logic flaws.

Q: Are you a registered company?
A: Sole proprietorship registered.

Q: What's the pricing?
A: Pilot at ₹3-5L for 3 months, credited toward the annual contract.

messages.author: Adithya
messages.content: Phase 1-3 complete. Juice Shop gate passed — 251 findings, 0 vendor FP. Moving to business track. Priority: Udyam registration TODAY.

messages.author: Adithya
messages.content: Venture Dock call done. Filed as warm contact. CEO impressed, no technical objections. Wrong geography for current program. Standing invite when raising. Do not follow up until first client closed.

docs.name: Engagement Contract Template
docs.description: Standard pilot engagement contract. Add liability caps and source code sovereignty clause before first use.

docs.name: NDA Template
docs.description: Mutual NDA for prospect conversations. Governs source code access during 48-hr PoV.

inbox.content: Follow up with DSCI / NASSCOM for direct CISO access events in Bengaluru / Mumbai.
Look into CERT-In empaneled audit firms — potential channel partner for BFSI distribution.
